title: Lifecycle PR Review Policy Configuration
subtitle: Make PR review a lifecycle-start policy, not an implicit routing accident.
scope: Standalone living-doc harness lifecycle policy, PR-review routing, closure gates, run contracts, dashboard visibility, and standalone proof runs.
sourceCoverage: Created from the May 11, 2026 session decision that PR-review behavior must be configured when starting the lifecycle controller, with three explicit modes: disabled, required-before-closure, and required-when-source-changes. GitHub epic #253 and implementation tickets #254-#263 were created from the acceptance criteria before implementation starts.
objective: Implement lifecycle-start policy configuration for the standalone living-doc harness so a run can explicitly choose whether PR review is disabled, always required before closure, or required only when controller evidence shows source/doc-relevant changes. The controller must carry that policy through run config, worker/reviewer contracts, evidence, routing, side-effect gates, closure review, dashboard artifacts, and lifecycle results. When PR review is required by policy, the lifecycle cannot close until a contract-bound pr-review inference unit has inspected the relevant evidence and produced an approved or not-required result. When PR review is disabled, the controller must not invoke pr-review and must not fabricate PR-review evidence for closure.
successCondition: A standalone harness run can be started in each PR review policy mode, proves distinct behavior for disabled, required-before-closure, and required-when-source-changes, preserves the selected policy in lifecycle artifacts and inference-unit contracts, routes to pr-review only when policy requires it, blocks closure when required PR evidence is missing, allows closure when required PR evidence is satisfied, displays the policy state in the dashboard, and proves both PR-enabled and PR-disabled paths through contract tests and standalone lifecycle runs.

runState.nextObjectiveAction: Lifecycle controller must rerun the configured proof routes and standalone PR-enabled/PR-disabled lifecycle runs, proving that required-before-closure PR-review is only invoked at the closure boundary.

basis: Worker-owned hardening completed on 2026-05-12T05:42:14.000Z: required PR-review policy gates and closure-review approval now require a paired pr-review result path and validation path before sideEffectEvidence.prReview can satisfy, block, or request the PR gate as contract-bound evidence. This closes the remaining fabricated-evidence shape where an injected approved pr-review output-contract-looking result path without validation could satisfy required PR policy. Focused worker-safe verification passed: node --check scripts/living-doc-harness-lifecycle.mjs, node --check scripts/living-doc-harness-iteration.mjs, node --check scripts/living-doc-harness-closure-review.mjs, and node tests/contract/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.spec.mjs. This worker did not run finalizer, reviewer, evidence-dashboard, lifecycle-control, proof-route, or controller-owned standalone lifecycle proof commands, so criterion-standalone-proof-runs remains controller-owned and unproven.

Worker-owned hardening completed on 2026-05-12T05:36:30.000Z: PR-review side-effect ingestion now requires the paired pr-review validation artifact to have ok true before sideEffectEvidence.prReview can satisfy or block the policy gate. Live harness run ldhl-20260512T032750Z-living-doc-pr-review-policy-configuration exposed a policy semantic drift: required-before-closure PR-review was invoked while the living doc was still repairable, objectiveReady=false, and acceptance criteria were incomplete. Issue #269 now scopes the correction: missing PR-review evidence may route to pr-review at closure-candidate/closed boundaries, but repairable/resumable work must continue objective work or repair instead of interrupting with PR-review.

proofRoutes.description: Full contract proof for lifecycle policy parsing, propagation, routing, and closure gates.
Focused E2E proof that the three policy modes produce distinct lifecycle graphs and dashboard evidence.
Standalone proof that required PR review prevents closure until pr-review contract evidence exists.
Standalone proof that disabled PR review never invokes pr-review and still closes through normal proof gates.

objectiveFacets.name: Lifecycle policy surface
objectiveFacets.description: The lifecycle controller accepts explicit policy configuration at run start instead of deriving PR-review behavior from scattered evidence flags.

objectiveFacets.name: Three PR policy modes
objectiveFacets.description: disabled, required-before-closure, and required-when-source-changes have distinct controller behavior.

objectiveFacets.name: Policy is separate from availability
objectiveFacets.description: allowedUnitTypes says what can run; prReviewPolicy says whether pr-review must run before closure.

objectiveFacets.name: Contract-bound PR review
objectiveFacets.description: PR review runs as an inference unit with explicit input contract, evidence paths, prompt, result, validation, and side-effect state.

objectiveFacets.name: Closure policy enforcement
objectiveFacets.description: Closure review must receive and enforce PR policy state and reject closure when required PR evidence is absent.

objectiveFacets.name: Dashboard policy observability
objectiveFacets.description: The dashboard exposes which policy was active and whether the PR gate was disabled, required, satisfied, or blocking.

objectiveFacets.name: Standalone proof
objectiveFacets.description: The objective is proven by standalone harness runs, not by this supervising session's judgment.

invariants.statement: Inference may judge PR-review quality, but it must not decide whether a configured mandatory PR review can be skipped.
Including pr-review in allowedUnitTypes makes it available; it does not make it mandatory unless prReviewPolicy requires it.
When prReviewPolicy.mode is disabled, the controller must not route to pr-review and must not fabricate PR-review evidence for closure.
When policy requires PR review, terminal closure is invalid until pr-review output contract evidence is approved or explicitly not-required.
The objective can close only from standalone lifecycle evidence, contract tests, and dashboard artifacts, not from supervising-session confidence.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.rationale: Shows the current stage and remaining proof gate.

data.name: Objective shaped
data.summary: The PR-review policy implementation now separates three concerns: policy availability, gate outcome state, and closure-boundary timing. Required PR-review evidence must now carry both a pr-review result artifact path and the paired validation artifact path before it can satisfy or shape the PR gate for closure. The remaining proof is controller-owned standalone lifecycle execution for the PR-enabled and PR-disabled paths.
data.nextStep: Rerun the PR-enabled and PR-disabled standalone lifecycle proof paths and record that required-before-closure invokes PR-review only when the lifecycle is closure-ready.

sections.title: Policy Decisions
sections.rationale: Captures the semantic decisions that must remain stable while implementation changes.

data.name: Policy is configured at lifecycle start
data.decision: The lifecycle controller receives PR-review policy as run configuration before the first worker starts.
notes: This prevents post-hoc inference from deciding that review can be skipped when policy says it is mandatory.
The policy must be durable in lifecycle artifacts so later units and dashboards can inspect it.

data.name: Policy is not the same as allowed unit types
data.decision: allowedUnitTypes controls which registered unit types may run; prReviewPolicy controls whether pr-review must run before closure.
notes: A run can allow pr-review without requiring it.
A run cannot require pr-review unless pr-review is allowed.

data.name: Required-before-closure is a closure-boundary gate
data.decision: required-before-closure makes PR-review mandatory before terminal closure, not during ordinary repairable or resumable objective work.
notes: Missing PR-review evidence under required-before-closure may select pr-review when the reviewer classifies the lifecycle as closure-candidate or closed.
The same missing evidence must not select pr-review while the living doc remains repairable, resumable, objectiveReady=false, or acceptance criteria are still incomplete.
This keeps PR-review as a closure gate and prevents it from interrupting the objective execution loop before the work is ready to close.

ticketIds.title: Correct required-before-closure PR-review routing semantics

data.name: Inference judges review quality
data.decision: The pr-review inference unit judges whether the work passes review, but the deterministic controller decides whether the review unit is mandatory from policy and hard facts.
notes: This preserves intelligence where it belongs while keeping mandatory gates enforceable.

data.name: Gate outcomes are first-class controller state
data.decision: Policy and side-effect gates must be represented as explicit outcomes: missing, requested, satisfied, or blocked. A blocked gate is real evidence, not missing evidence.
notes: This prevents the controller from repeatedly invoking the same gate unit after that unit already produced a blocked output contract.
The invariant applies first to pr-review and should generalize to other controller-owned gates such as commit intent or closure review.
A blocked pr-review result must route to continuation/blocker reasoning with the blocked result path, not another identical pr-review unit.

ticketIds.title: Add gate outcome state model for controller routing
Process defect: blocked PR-review unit loops instead of producing a terminal blocker or satisfying evidence

sections.title: PR Review Policy Modes
sections.rationale: Defines the operational modes the lifecycle controller must implement.

data.name: disabled
data.summary: PR review is never invoked. Closure can proceed through normal commit and closure-review gates, but no PR-review evidence is required or invented.

data.name: required-before-closure
data.summary: PR review is mandatory before closure whenever the run reaches closure-candidate or closed state, independent of whether changed files are present.
outputs: Controller routes to pr-review before closure-review if PR evidence is missing
Closure review rejects terminal closure without approved or not-required PR output contract

data.name: required-when-source-changes
data.summary: PR review is mandatory only when controller-owned hard facts show source/doc-relevant changes in the lifecycle.
inputs: controller evidence sourceFilesChanged or relevant source/doc changes
outputs: Controller routes to pr-review when relevant changes exist and PR evidence is missing
Controller skips pr-review when no relevant changes exist

sections.title: Acceptance Criteria
sections.rationale: Defines objective-tight closure gates for the PR-review policy implementation.

data.name: Lifecycle start accepts explicit PR policy
data.objectiveTerm: a run can explicitly choose whether PR review is disabled, always required before closure, or required only when controller evidence shows source/doc-relevant changes
data.criterion: The lifecycle controller accepts a first-class prReviewPolicy at run start with exactly the supported modes disabled, required-before-closure, and required-when-source-changes.
data.proofRequired: CLI/API/run config artifact shows prReviewPolicy as explicit lifecycle policy and rejects unknown modes.
data.acceptanceTest: Contract tests prove valid modes parse and invalid modes fail before the first worker starts.

ticketIds.title: Add lifecycle-start PR review policy configuration

data.name: Policy is separate from unit availability
data.objectiveTerm: policy configuration for the standalone living-doc harness
data.criterion: allowedUnitTypes and prReviewPolicy are separate run configuration fields with separate validation semantics.
data.proofRequired: Tests show pr-review can be allowed but not required, and that requiring pr-review fails when pr-review is not allowed.
data.acceptanceTest: A disabled run with pr-review allowed never invokes pr-review; a required run without pr-review allowed fails configuration validation.

ticketIds.title: Separate PR review policy from allowed inference unit types

data.name: Disabled mode never invokes PR review
data.objectiveTerm: When PR review is disabled, the controller must not invoke pr-review and must not fabricate PR-review evidence for closure.
data.criterion: In disabled mode, the controller never selects pr-review and closure-review receives policy evidence showing PR review is disabled.
data.proofRequired: Lifecycle selection artifacts, graph artifacts, and closure-review input contain no selected pr-review unit and no fake PR side-effect evidence.
data.acceptanceTest: A disabled-mode lifecycle can close through normal gates without any pr-review unit in the graph.

ticketIds.title: Implement disabled PR review policy mode

data.name: Required-before-closure blocks closure until PR evidence exists
data.objectiveTerm: When PR review is required by policy, the lifecycle cannot close until a contract-bound pr-review inference unit has inspected the relevant evidence and produced an approved or not-required result.
data.criterion: In required-before-closure mode, a closure-candidate or closed reviewer verdict routes to pr-review before terminal closure when PR evidence is missing, while repairable or resumable verdicts continue objective work or repair instead of selecting pr-review only because PR evidence is absent.
data.proofRequired: Post-review selection and lifecycle graph show pr-review selected at the closure boundary, not during repairable/resumable work; closure-review rejects missing PR evidence.
data.acceptanceTest: A required-before-closure contract test proves repairable + missing PR evidence routes to worker/continuation, not pr-review; a closure-candidate + missing PR evidence test routes to pr-review; the same path with approved or not-required PR output can proceed to closure-review.

ticketIds.title: Implement required-before-closure PR review policy mode
Correct required-before-closure PR-review routing semantics

data.name: Required-when-source-changes follows controller hard facts
data.objectiveTerm: required only when controller evidence shows source/doc-relevant changes
data.criterion: In required-when-source-changes mode, the controller invokes pr-review only when controller-owned hard facts show source/doc-relevant changes.
data.proofRequired: Controller evidence snapshot records the change facts used to require or skip PR review.
data.acceptanceTest: Two tests prove distinct behavior: changed source/doc files require pr-review; no relevant changes skip pr-review.

ticketIds.title: Implement required-when-source-changes PR review policy mode

data.name: PR review receives a complete contract
data.objectiveTerm: contract-bound pr-review inference unit has inspected the relevant evidence
data.criterion: The worker/reviewer contracts carry run policy, and the pr-review unit input contract includes the policy mode, living doc path, evidence snapshot path, reviewer verdict path, changed files or commit evidence when applicable, required hard facts, and required inspection paths.
data.proofRequired: Worker, reviewer, and pr-review input-contract.json and validation.json artifacts prove all policy fields are present.
data.acceptanceTest: Contract tests reject pr-review input missing policy, evidence snapshot, reviewer verdict, or required inspection paths.

ticketIds.title: Carry complete policy context into PR review input contracts

data.name: Closure review enforces PR policy
data.objectiveTerm: The controller must carry that policy through run config, worker/reviewer contracts, evidence, routing, side-effect gates, closure review, dashboard artifacts, and lifecycle results.
data.criterion: Closure-review input and result validation include PR policy state and reject terminal closure when policy-required PR evidence is absent.
data.proofRequired: Closure-review input contract includes prReviewPolicy and prReviewRequired, plus sideEffectEvidence.prReview or a valid not-required result when needed.
data.acceptanceTest: Closure-review tests prove required PR evidence blocks closure and satisfied PR evidence allows closure.

ticketIds.title: Enforce PR review policy in closure review

data.name: Dashboard exposes policy state
data.criterion: The dashboard shows the selected PR policy mode and the PR gate state for each lifecycle run.
data.proofRequired: Dashboard graph API and UI inspector expose disabled, required, satisfied, missing, or blocked PR gate state from lifecycle artifacts.
data.acceptanceTest: Dashboard contract or E2E test opens the lifecycle graph and verifies policy mode and PR gate status are visible.

ticketIds.title: Expose PR review policy state in the harness dashboard

data.name: Contract tests cover all policy modes
data.objectiveTerm: proves distinct behavior for disabled, required-before-closure, and required-when-source-changes
data.criterion: The contract suite covers each mode, invalid configuration, missing PR evidence, satisfied PR evidence, and required policy with pr-review not allowed.
data.proofRequired: npm run test:contract passes with focused PR policy assertions.
data.acceptanceTest: The focused contract tests fail if any mode collapses into another mode's behavior.

ticketIds.title: Add contract tests for PR review policy modes

data.name: Standalone runs prove enabled and disabled paths
data.objectiveTerm: proves both PR-enabled and PR-disabled paths through contract tests and standalone lifecycle runs
data.criterion: At least one standalone run proves a PR-required path and one standalone run proves a PR-disabled path from lifecycle-result.json, graph artifacts, inference-unit contracts, and closure-review evidence.
data.proofRequired: Two .living-doc-runs lifecycle-result.json artifacts show closed final states with the expected presence or absence of pr-review units.
data.acceptanceTest: The living doc records the run IDs and proof paths before the objective can be marked closed.

ticketIds.title: Prove PR review policy with standalone lifecycle runs

data.name: Gate outcome state prevents repeated gate loops
data.objectiveTerm: The controller must carry policy through routing, side-effect gates, closure review, dashboard artifacts, and lifecycle results.
data.criterion: The controller represents each policy/side-effect gate as missing, requested, satisfied, or blocked, and does not select the same gate unit again for a blocked gate unless new evidence changes the gate state.
data.proofRequired: Controller evidence, post-review selection, output-input contracts, and dashboard graph metadata expose prReviewGate.status and preserve blocked pr-review result paths as evidence.
data.acceptanceTest: A lifecycle with a blocked pr-review output contract routes to continuation-inference or an explicit blocked state, not another pr-review unit; dashboard graph shows the blocked gate and result artifact.

ticketIds.title: Add gate outcome state model for controller routing
Process defect: blocked PR-review unit loops instead of producing a terminal blocker or satisfying evidence

sections.title: Relationship Maps
sections.rationale: Makes the policy/control relationship inspectable before implementation starts. The diagrams are not proof; the acceptance criteria define what must become true.

data.name: Policy vs inference judgment
data.claim: Policy decides whether PR review is mandatory; inference decides whether the work passes PR review; the controller enforces both through contracts.
data.validationQuestion: Can the run prove that mandatory review came from lifecycle policy and review quality came from the pr-review inference unit?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if reviewer or closure-review can skip a configured mandatory PR review by semantic judgment alone.

diagrams.title: Policy and Judgment Boundary
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Start["Lifecycle start"] --> Policy["prReviewPolicy"]
  Policy --> Controller["Deterministic controller"]
  Evidence["Controller hard facts"] --> Controller
  Controller -->|policy requires review| PRUnit["pr-review inference unit"]
  PRUnit --> ReviewResult["approved / changes-requested / blocked / not-required"]
  ReviewResult --> Controller
  Controller --> ClosureReview["closure-review"]
  Controller -->|policy disabled| ClosureReview
  ClosureReview --> Terminal["terminal closure only if policy gate satisfied"]

data.name: Three PR policy modes
data.claim: The three modes are distinct lifecycle behaviors, not aliases for the same routing flag.
data.validationQuestion: Do tests and lifecycle artifacts prove distinct route behavior for disabled, required-before-closure, and required-when-source-changes?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if the system treats allowed pr-review as required, treats required-before-closure as a repair-loop gate, or treats required-when-source-changes as always required.

diagrams.title: Mode Routing
diagrams.text:
flowchart TD
  Mode["prReviewPolicy.mode"] --> Disabled["disabled"]
  Mode --> Always["required-before-closure"]
  Mode --> Changed["required-when-source-changes"]
  Disabled --> NoPR["Never select pr-review"]
  Always --> Boundary{"closure-candidate or closed?"}
  Boundary -->|no| Continue["Continue objective work / repair"]
  Boundary -->|yes and PR evidence missing| NeedPR["Select pr-review before closure"]
  Changed --> Facts{"source/doc-relevant changes?"}
  Facts -->|yes| NeedPR
  Facts -->|no| SkipPR["Skip pr-review"]
  NoPR --> Closure["Normal closure-review gates"]
  NeedPR --> PRUnit["pr-review contract"]
  PRUnit --> Closure
  SkipPR --> Closure

data.name: Gate outcome state machine
data.claim: A controller-owned gate is not binary. Missing, requested, satisfied, and blocked are distinct states with different next-unit routing obligations.
data.validationQuestion: Can the lifecycle prove that a blocked pr-review output becomes blocked gate evidence and cannot immediately select pr-review again without new evidence?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if blocked PR-review output is treated as absent evidence or if the controller repeats the same gate unit for the same blocked state.

ticketIds.title: Add gate outcome state model for controller routing
Process defect: blocked PR-review unit loops instead of producing a terminal blocker or satisfying evidence

diagrams.title: Gate Outcome Routing
diagrams.text:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Missing
  Missing --> Requested: controller selects gate unit
  Requested --> Satisfied: unit returns approved/not-required
  Requested --> Blocked: unit returns blocked
  Satisfied --> ClosureReview: gate can support closure
  Blocked --> Continuation: explain blocker and choose non-repeating next move
  Blocked --> Requested: only if new evidence changes gate state
  Continuation --> [*]

diagrams.title: PR Review Loop Guard
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Reviewer["reviewer: pr-review-policy-gate-missing"] --> Controller["controller selection"]
  Controller --> PR["pr-review unit"]
  PR --> Blocked["outputContract.status = blocked"]
  Blocked --> Evidence["prReviewGate.status = blocked"]
  Evidence --> Guard{"new evidence changed gate?"}
  Guard -->|no| Continue["continuation-inference / blocker"]
  Guard -->|yes| PR
  Continue --> Dashboard["dashboard shows blocked gate + resultPath"]

sections.title: Verification Plan
sections.rationale: Names the proof surfaces needed before this living doc can honestly close.

data.name: Contract suite
data.currentCoverage: npm run test:contract passed on 2026-05-12T04:01:52.000Z. Focused contract tests now cover policy validation, separated allowed-unit semantics, iteration PR gates, lifecycle controller routing, policy propagation, closure blocking/satisfaction, dashboard graph metadata, source-change-gated PR routing in both directions, URL-only or result-path-only PR metadata rejection, closure-review inspection of the satisfying PR-review output contract path, invalid policy mode rejection, dashboard lifecycle-start API propagation, lifecycle result path references for runs created outside the repo, the gate outcome loop guard where blocked pr-review output routes to continuation-inference instead of another pr-review, and the closure-boundary semantics where required-before-closure does not invoke pr-review for repairable work but does invoke it for closure-candidate work when PR evidence is missing.
data.nextStep: Record the controller-owned standalone lifecycle proof routes, including proof that required-before-closure does not invoke PR-review before closure readiness.

data.name: Standalone lifecycle proof
data.currentCoverage: Implementation and focused fixture runs prove routing behavior, including closure-boundary PR-review routing, but controller-owned standalone proof still has to validate the behavior in a full lifecycle.
data.nextStep: The lifecycle controller must run this living doc through the harness and record both PR-enabled and PR-disabled lifecycle results. The PR-enabled run must prove PR-review is not invoked while the doc remains repairable/resumable and is invoked only when closure is otherwise available.
gaps: Standalone required-before-closure run artifact is not recorded yet.
Standalone disabled run artifact is not recorded yet.
Standalone closure-boundary PR-review routing proof is not recorded yet.

sections.title: Scope Boundaries
sections.rationale: Prevents this objective from expanding into full GitHub PR automation or review-quality heuristics before policy enforcement is proven.

data.name: No full GitHub PR automation in this slice
data.decision: Actually opening or updating a GitHub PR can remain simulated, blocked, or handled by a later side-effect policy.
notes: This objective is about lifecycle policy, routing, contracts, and closure enforcement.

data.name: No deep PR review rubric in this slice
data.decision: Full PR-review quality heuristics are not part of this objective.
notes: The pr-review unit must receive the right evidence and produce a valid contract, but improving its semantic rubric can be a later objective.
